title: Parahebe brevistylis
taxonomy: Parahebe brevistylis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. It belongs to the genus Parahebe, which is a group of plants native to New Zealand. The genus Parahebe is further classified under the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. Parahebe brevistylis is a rare and endemic species found in the southern part of New Zealand's South Island. Its classification is part of the ongoing efforts to understand and categorize the diverse plant life of New Zealand.

intro: Parahebe brevistylis, commonly known as the short-styled Parahebe, is a flowering plant species in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including gastrointestinal problems and fever, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Parahebe brevistylis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
